caption: The image features a close-up of former U.S. President Barack Obama smiling broadly. Superimposed over the image are bold, white text captions that read "NORTH CAROLINA BANNED GAY MARRIAGE?" at the top and "SWEET! NOW I CAN MAKE THIS ELECTION ABOUT SOCIAL ISSUES INSTEAD OF MY COMPLETE AND UTTER FAILURE AT MANAGING THE ECONOMY!!!!" at the bottom. The image has a meme-like format with a watermark in the lower right corner.

The image is a meme featuring two sections: the top shows a quote attributed to Donald Trump about illegal immigrants, accompanied by a picture of him speaking. The bottom section displays a photo of Barack Obama with the caption “OH WAIT, THAT WAS THIS GUY...”, implying a humorous contrast or misattribution.

The image shows two men, Barack Obama and Vladimir Putin, depicted on a split screen, each holding a phone to their ear. Below the images is a humorous dialogue between the two characters, presented as if they are having a conversation. The image includes a copyright notice for SatireWire.com dated 2016.

The image features a smiling Barack Obama standing at a podium with microphones. Text is overlaid in bold, white capital letters, reading "THE MORE TAXES YOU PAY" at the top and "THE MORE FREE STUFF YOU GET FROM THE GOVERNMENT" at the bottom. The background shows blurred event signage and a colorful, patterned border framing the image.

The image is a meme featuring two side-by-side panels. The top panel shows a photo of Osama bin Laden with text claiming Obama called the SEALs and they got him. The bottom panel shows a photo of Barack Obama on the phone with text jokingly claiming the SEALs called Obama and he denied them.

The image is a three-panel meme featuring former President Barack Obama and his wife Michelle Obama seated in an audience. In the top panel, Michelle holds a small photo while Obama looks ahead. The middle panel shows them seated next to each other with text overlay, and the bottom panel shows them switched seats with the caption “YOU. SWITCH. SEATS.”

The image shows two men in suits shaking hands while seated. Text overlaid on the image reads, "WHEN TEACHER WOULD MAKE YOU SHAKE HANDS WITH THE KID YOU HATE!" A logo for "RVCJ" and the website "www.rvcj.com" are visible in the top right corner.

The image shows two men, Barack Obama and Joe Biden, seated next to each other against a red, white, and blue striped backdrop. A tweet overlay on the right side reads, “HE FINALLY MADE HIS OWN MEME!! I miss the Obama/Joe friendship memes so much it almost hurts.”

The image displays two side-by-side panels with the heading "DEMOCRATS IN 1860:" above each. The left panel shows a black-and-white photograph of a Black man wearing a chain collar, with the quote: "They’re not persons under the law. They have no rights." The right panel contains an incomplete quote and a blurred image of a fiery explosion.

The image is a black-and-white meme featuring a crowd of people with two men standing on a raised platform. Large text at the top reads "THE DEMOCRAT PARTY," and below the image, additional text states "PRO-CHOICE SINCE 1828" followed by "IT'S NOT REALLY MURDER WHEN YOU CHOOSE TO BELIEVE THEY'RE NOT REALLY PEOPLE." The bottom of the image includes a caption: "It's time to stop being afraid you'll offend someone by sharing the truth. ~Brian."

The image features a close-up of Barack Obama pointing directly at the viewer. Superimposed white text reads, “I WANT YOU TO BECOME A BETTER MAN...SO JOIN DEMOLAY!” The background is blurred, focusing attention on Obama’s face and the text.

The image is a collage of nine headshots showing various U.S. political figures with their mouths open as if shouting or speaking passionately. Below the images is a bold text statement accusing the Democratic Party of radicalization and ending with the hashtag #WALKAWAY. The overall design resembles a political protest or meme format.

The image is a four-panel meme comparing how the Norwegian Social Democratic Party presents its leader across different social media platforms. Each panel shows a different photo of the same man, with labels indicating the platform: LinkedIn (formal portrait), Facebook (smiling in a beanie), Instagram (diving into water), and Tinder (holding a phone). The caption at the bottom identifies the party as “The Norwegian social democratic party.”

This is a screenshot of a Facebook post by Sue Gafford Piner, featuring a meme image of an older man with overlaid text. The text reads: “I PAY WHITE COPS TO MURDER BLACK PEOPLE. AND THEN I PAY BLACK PEOPLE TO RIOT. BECAUSE RACE WARS KEEP THE SHEEP IN LINE.” Below the image, a comment by Steve Baysinger attributes the statement to “George Soros” and adds, “My theory only. No supporting evidence. What do YOU think?” Another user, Donna J Greenwalt, replies with a “Yep.”

The image is a meme split into two sections. The top section shows a historical painting of Louis XIV with the text “THE RISE OF ABSOLUTISM IN FRANCE” and his quote “L’etat, c’est moi (I am the state).” The bottom section features a photo of Joe Biden smiling, with the caption “I AM THE DEMOCRATIC PARTY” and the date “-JOE BIDEN 9/29/2020.”

The image displays a CNN Travel news article headline about a large water park party in Wuhan, juxtaposed with a photo of actor Morgan Freeman pointing upward. Below the headline, a meme-style text overlay reads, “THEY DON’T HAVE A DEMOCRATIC PARTY DESPERATE TO WIN AN ELECTION.” The overall composition combines a news report with a satirical image and caption.

This is the cover of TIME magazine featuring a close-up black-and-white portrait of Donald Trump. His eyes are digitally altered to appear red, and four checkboxes with red checkmarks are superimposed over his face. Each box is labeled with a word: “bully,” “showman,” “party crasher,” and “demagogue,” with the text “the 45th President of the United States” appearing to the right.

The image displays three U.S. politicians—Cornel West, Elizabeth Warren, and Mitt Romney—in a panel discussion setting, with a speech bubble above Romney that reads "GOO GOO GA GA!" The background features a red, white, and blue design with stars, and text at the top reads "If unborn children could vote. Democratic Party:". The image is overlaid with the watermark "Christian theology memes."

The image shows a close-up of Representative Devin Nunes with a quote overlay. The quote attributes him with stating that while no collusion was found between Russia and the Trump campaign, links were found between the Clinton campaign and the Democratic Party to the Russians. Below the quote, text summarizes his statement from "Sunday Morning Futures" regarding findings from his committee.

This is a screenshot of a Twitter post by user Hasan Hammett. The tweet features a split image: on the left, a black-and-white photo of a group of people with a cross, accompanied by text claiming the KKK was created as the militant wing of the Democratic Party; on the right, a close-up photo of Hillary Clinton. The tweet includes hashtags like #DemocratLies, #LiberalAgenda, #BlackLivesMatter, and #KeepGodFirst.

The image features a photo of Donald Trump smiling, overlaid with bold text that reads "WHEN EVERYONE CALLING YOU RACIST IS A PEDOPHILE". Below the photo, there is a block of text that accuses Democratic officials of sexual assault and pedophilia allegations and claims this signifies the end of the Democratic Party. The image also includes a credit to "Creativehobby.store".

The image is a meme featuring photos of Senator Kamala Harris and Senator Cory Booker. Above their images is a text exchange depicting a fictional conversation between a U.S. voter and the Democratic Party, which ends with the voter saying “Even better!” Below the photos, the caption reads “The Idiocy of Democratic Voters.”

The image features a portrait of Tomi Lahren against a nighttime city skyline background. A quote attributed to her is displayed in large text, stating, “The Democratic Party used to be about working class Americans. Now it’s about illegal immigrants.” Below the quote, there is additional text noting that she made this statement on Fox News’ @foxandfriends.

The image shows a group of ten people standing on a stage with a large, curved backdrop displaying stars. Text at the top reads, “ONE LARGE STAGE. NOT ONE USA FLAG.” Additional text at the bottom states, “THAT SAYS MORE ABOUT THE SOCIALISTS RUNNING TODAY’S DEMOCRAT PARTY THAN ANY ESSAY OR SPEECH EVER WILL.”

The image features Donald Trump with his hair dramatically windswept, appearing as if blown backward by strong wind. White bold text at the top reads “WE SHALL OVER COMB” and at the bottom “TRUMP 2016,” forming a pun on “we shall overcome.” A person in a green cap is partially visible behind Trump’s right shoulder.

The image is a meme featuring a split-screen comparison of Donald Trump and the character Chucky from the horror movie franchise. Above the split image, the text reads "Donald Trump COINCIDENCE?". Below the split image, the text says "I DON'T THINK SO MEMES", with "MEMES" displayed in rainbow-colored letters.

A woman with short gray hair, wearing a blue blazer over a white top, stands smiling and gesturing with her right hand. Behind her is a large pile of U.S. dollar bills. The text at the top reads "The GREEN party" and at the bottom includes a Facebook URL.

The image shows an elderly woman lying on a bed of U.S. dollar bills, holding more money in her hands. A text overlay reads “NOW I KNOW WHY THEY CALL IT THE GREEN PARTY,” with “JDC” written in the corner. Below the image, text states “Crowdfunding for election recounts - $6,000,000 Getting to keep the money - Priceless.”

The image displays a map of North Africa and the Middle East with several countries shaded in red. At the top, bold red text reads, "BREAKING NEWS: GREEN PARTY-FUNDED RECOUNTS HAVE AWARDED THE FOLLOWING STATES TO HILLARY CLINTON!!" The map includes labels for countries such as Algeria, Libya, Egypt, and Saudi Arabia. A small logo is visible in the bottom right corner.

The image displays the "Feels Good Man" meme character against a dark background. Text at the top reads "WHAT LIBERTARIAN AND GREEN PARTY FEEL LIKE," and below the character, "I hardly ever hear of these guys" is written, followed by a list of other political affiliations.

The image shows a black-and-white photograph of a shirtless, muscular man with a mustache, striking a powerful pose with clenched fists. He is looking off to the side with a stern expression. Text overlays at the top and bottom read: “THE DEMOCRATIC PARTY?” and “YOU MEAN THAT THING WOMEN AND GAYS LIKE?”

A man with a beard and straw hat sits indoors, wearing a blue shirt, with a wooden-paneled background and windows visible behind him. The image includes overlaid text reading “Yearning for a libertarian utopia while relying entirely on your parents” and “#justneckbeardthings.” Below, additional text says, “When statist normies won’t leave me alone and let m’superior intellect and abilities shine.”

The image shows Donald Trump with a smug expression, wearing a dark suit, white shirt, and red tie. Text at the top reads, “HOW ARE WE BREAKING THE DEMOCRATIC PARTY??” and at the bottom, “BY ACTUALLY HELPING THE PEOPLE THEY PROMISE TO HELP EVERY ELECTION!” The background appears to be a stage with a microphone.

The image displays a Facebook post by Sue Gafford Piner featuring a meme with a photo of George Soros overlaid with bold text. The text reads: “I PAY WHITE COPS TO MURDER BLACK PEOPLE. AND THEN I PAY BLACK PEOPLE TO RIOT. BECAUSE RACE WARS KEEP THE SHEEP IN LINE.” Below the image, Steve Baysinger comments, “George Soros. My theory only. No supporting evidence. What do YOU think?” and Donna J Greenwalt replies with a “Yep.”

The image shows a meme featuring Donald Trump on a phone call, looking annoyed. The text above depicts a caller asking for the "giant orange fuck" (a reference to Trump), to which Trump responds by correcting the caller that he is Donald Trump — not Biden — and then exclaiming, “THIS YOU AGAIN?!” before the caller hangs up.

This is a political advertisement featuring Joe Biden leaning down to comfort two children. The left side of the image has bold text reading “I’M ON TEAM JOE!” and “SNIFFING OUT COVID-19,” along with a call to action to text “JOE to 30330.” The bottom includes the “BIDEN PRESIDENT” logo and a website for creating a custom avatar.

The image features a close-up of Joe Biden’s face against a dark background. White text at the top reads “LEARN TO CODE” and at the bottom reads “JOE BIDEN.” He appears to be looking upward with a slightly concerned or surprised expression.

The image is a collage of six photos, arranged in two columns and three rows. The left column shows a man kissing a woman on the cheek, while the right column displays two different photos of the same man kissing different women, each time with a red label underneath. All photos are labeled with text indicating whether they are “PHOTOSHOPPED” or “NOT PHOTOSHOPPED.”

The image is a comparison graphic titled "Bernie or Biden?" focusing on healthcare policy. It features portraits of Bernie Sanders and Joe Biden, with Sanders' proposal labeled as "We will provide medicare for all people at no cost" and Biden's as "ObamaCare 2.0 will likely be called JoeCare." The graphic includes the tagline "Be informed. Compare them on the issues that matter."

The image shows a man in a gray military-style jacket with his left hand raised, appearing to gesture while speaking. Text at the top reads, "WHY BIDEN NO GET VIRUS?". A portion of another person is visible on the right edge of the frame.

The image is a vertically stacked collage of three photographs, each showing a man receiving a medal from another man. Large white letters spelling "RAPE" are on the left side and "TEAM" are on the right side, framing the images. The backgrounds in each photo feature official settings with American flags.

The image shows a person wearing a white, hooded garment with the word "HANES" visible on the hood, and a white face mask pulled down below their chin. The text at the top reads, “JOE KNOWS HOW TO STAY SAFE FROM THE CORONA VIRUS,” and at the bottom, “JOE BIDEN 2020!” The individual is seated inside a vehicle, with the car’s interior visible in the background.

This is a CNN news broadcast featuring two men in suits. On the left, a news anchor looks toward the camera, while on the right, President Joe Biden rests his hand near his mouth. A graphic overlay at the bottom reads "BREAKING NEWS: JOE BIDEN SPEAKS TO CNN ABOUT CORONAVIRUS PANDEMIC," with a pandemic data panel in the upper right corner.

The image shows two men seated side by side in formal suits. The man on the right covers his face with his hands, while the man on the left sits with his hands clasped. A sculpted bust is visible on a pedestal behind them.

The image shows a bald man with a beard wearing a suit and tie, with a large headline above him that reads “I WALKED AWAY FROM THE REPUBLICANS.” Below the headline, a text block states he renounces his membership in the Republican Party, calling it corrupt and immoral, and blames its leaders for disgracing the country and the Party of Lincoln. The text also identifies him as Steve Schmidt, former GOP strategist, with a caption at the bottom reading “Here’s one of the sane, moral ones.”

A black-and-white image shows two hands clasped in a firm handshake. Below the image, a caption reads: “GOOD IDEAS DON’T REQUIRE FORCE” followed by the Libertarian Party logo and a message thanking the Libertarian Party of Florida and directing viewers to lp.org/states.

The image displays a man in a suit and tie, identified as Gary Johnson, standing at a podium. Superimposed over the image is a quote attributed to him: “HERE IS WHAT YOU CAN EXPECT FROM OUR ADMINISTRATION: HUMILITY AND HONESTY. NOT ARROGANCE ABOUT THE POWER OF THE STATE.” The text uses varied colors for emphasis, with “HUMILITY AND HONESTY” in yellow and “ARROGANCE” in red.

The image displays a green background with text criticizing libertarians as elitists who believe they can run their lives better than the government. A black-and-white illustration of a man in a top hat and suit, holding a cane, is positioned to the right. At the bottom, there is a credit to the Clark County Indiana Libertarian Party and a link to their website.

The image displays campaign materials for the Libertarian Party of New York's 2020 election efforts, featuring a central depiction of the Statue of Liberty. Surrounding the statue are six framed portraits of candidates with their names and positions, including Jo Jorgenson (President), US Senate, US House, and Public Service roles. The design uses a yellow and gray gradient background with the party's name and year prominently displayed.

The image features a collage of five U.S. presidents—George H.W. Bush, Bill Clinton, George W. Bush, Barack Obama, and Donald Trump—set against a fiery background with fighter jets flying overhead. Above them, bold text reads, "NO MATTER WHO YOU VOTE FOR, THE RICH STAY RICH, THE POOR STAY POOR," and below, "AND THE BOMBS NEVER STOP." A credit at the bottom thanks "the Libertarian Party of Florida for this post!"

The image is a collage of six meme panels, featuring Donald Trump and Hillary Clinton with exaggerated captions criticizing and mocking both figures. Each panel includes bold, often offensive text such as "MEXICANS ARE RAPISTS!", "TRUMP!", and "HILLARY IS SO OFFENSIVE!", paired with photos of the politicians. The layout presents a satirical, inflammatory tone through text and imagery.

The image features a close-up portrait of Donald Trump Jr., with a quote overlaid on the right side. The quote reads: “Russians make up a pretty disproportionate cross-section of a lot of our assets. We see a lot of money pouring in from Russia.” The quote is attributed to Donald Trump Jr., 2008.

The image shows a close-up of a police officer's uniform, focusing on a black utility belt with various equipment, including a radio and other gear. A patch on the belt reads "THE BEATINGS WILL CONTINUE." The officer stands in front of a street with a visible crosswalk.

The image shows a gazelle standing atop a zebra in an enclosure, with a clear blue sky and a fence visible in the background. Superimposed text reads “THIS IS WHAT LIBERTARIANS BELIEVE” at the top and “AMERICANS AGAINST THE LIBERTARIAN PARTY” at the bottom. A red prohibition symbol overlays a party logo in the lower right corner.

The image shows a television screen displaying a news segment with Nancy Fichandler, who is speaking while gesturing with her right hand. The show's logo, "IN THE KNOW," appears in the top-left and bottom-right corners. A YouTube-style overlay at the bottom indicates the video is titled "Should The Government Stop Dumping Money Into A Giant Hole?" with 1.8M views and posted 11 years ago.

The image features a smiling portrait of Hillary Clinton against a background with an American flag. Overlaid text reads, "The ONLY Bridges hillary's built Are EXIT Ramps," followed by party labels pointing to different exits: "Green Party Exit LEFT," "Libertarian Party Next Exit," "Republican Party Exit RIGHT," and a final line stating, "Any Republican Can NOW beat her."

The image features a person with dark, curly hair against a snowy background. Text overlaid on the image reads "I'M NOT SITH I'M ALT-JEDI YOU CULTURAL REBEL CUCK". Below the image, a caption states: "No, 'Clovergender' is NOT a Thing! - Libertarian Party of Nevada".

The image shows a young girl with blonde hair tied in a high ponytail, wearing a blue and green cheerleading uniform. She has an excited expression with her mouth open wide and is pointing forward with her right index finger. The image includes text above and below her that reads, “What someone looks like when” and “They are about to tell you how democratic socialism is different than socialism.”

The image is a political advertisement featuring a smiling young man in a blue shirt and tie on the left side. On the right, bold text asks, “DO YOU REALLY THINK THIS GUY CAN RUN YOUR LIFE BETTER THAN YOU CAN?” Below, it states, “Neither does he. That’s why he’s a Libertarian.” The bottom includes a call to action to join the Libertarian Party, along with their logo and website URLs.

The image features bold yellow text on a dark background with a silhouette of a person raising their arms. The text declares personal freedom as inherent and non-negotiable, asserting that rights are not subject to government removal. A faint watermark with social media handles is visible in the lower left corner.

The image features a man with distinctive blond hair blowing in the wind, wearing a dark suit, white shirt, and striped tie. Text overlays read: "THIS HIGHLY RATED SHIT-SHOW IS THE REASON WHY AMERICA IS LOW ON TOILET PAPER" and at the bottom, "Like a train wreck you just can't help watching." A watermark for "imgflip.com" is visible in the lower-left corner.

The image is a meme-style graphic comparing two figures: Sheldon Adelson, who donated $30 million and received a $670 million tax break, and the Koch Brothers, who donated $40 million and received a $500 million tax break. It features photos of each individual with their donation and tax break amounts listed below their images. The bottom of the image contains the caption: “THIS IS WHAT IT LOOKS LIKE WHEN BILLIONAIRES OWN YOUR GOVERNMENT.”

The image shows two men in suits, one with his hand on the other's shoulder, with a U.S. flag partially visible in the background. White bold text overlays the image, reading "THE REPUBLICAN PARTY WON'T BE ABLE TO GET ANYTHING BY US NOW." The man on the left has a somber expression while the man on the right is smiling.

The image displays two side-by-side portraits: on the left, a modern man with a slight smile, and on the right, a black-and-white photo of a man with exaggerated makeup and a mustache. Above them, text reads, “One of these men is a blood sucking vampire from a bygone era, and the other is Grandpa Munster.”

The image shows a person holding a handwritten sign that reads "DEPORT FASHISOM" in an outdoor nighttime setting. The person is wearing a dark beanie and a hooded jacket, with a crowd visible in the background. The image includes the text "LIBERAL COLLEGES" at the top and "WHERE THEY TEACH HOW TO HATE AMERICA BUT NOT HOW TO SPELL" at the bottom.

The image is a political poster with red text on a white background, framed in black. The top text reads, "Remember when people didn't vote because they said there was no difference between Hillary and Trump..." and the bottom text states, "And now there are child concentration camps." Attributed to Zach Bornstein and the Democratic Organizing for America, it features a photograph of several people walking near a large white tent in an open field.

The image shows a woman embracing a young boy in what appears to be a school setting, with classroom bulletin boards and other adults in the background. Two men stand to the left, one wearing a blue shirt and khaki pants. Text overlays at the top and bottom of the image read, "LIKE AND SHARE IF YOU WOULD LIKE TO SEE" and "AN ARMED VETERAN IN EVERY SCHOOL IN AMERICA."

The image features a man with a thoughtful expression, making a finger-to-temple gesture. Superimposed text reads: “YOU CAN’T VOTE MORE THAN ONCE WHEN THE REPUBLICAN PARTY VOTES FOR YOU.” Below, additional text states: “In their own effed up brains, Republican officials 'committed no wrong' by committing election fraud to 'prevent voter fraud'.”

The image shows a close-up of Donald Trump speaking, with his mouth open mid-sentence. To the left, a quote is superimposed: “I mean, part of the beauty of me is that I'm very honest. Most republicans act like they aren't racist.” The background is dark and blurred.

The image features a man with curly blonde hair, wearing a brown top hat, a purple velvet jacket, and a large beige bow tie. He has a smug expression, resting his head on his hand, against a blurred background. Text overlaid on the image reads: “HATE TO BREAK IT TO YOU” at the top and “BUT THE REPUBLICAN PARTY HASN’T DONE THIS COUNTRY ONE BIT OF GOOD” at the bottom.

A young child, dressed in a light-colored suit with a bow tie, stands indoors on a wooden floor while holding a small object. The image has text overlaid at the top and bottom, reading “TEACHER ASKED WHAT REPUBLICANS STAND FOR” and “I SAID AMERICA.” The child is smiling and looking toward the camera.

The image displays a close-up of Rob Reiner's face on the left side, with a quote attributed to him in bold white text on a dark background. The quote reads: “IF DONALD TRUMP WAS NOT A CELEBRITY, THE WORDS THAT COME OUT OF HIS MOUTH, YOU’D SEE A GUY, A LUNATIC IN THE PARK ON A SOAPBOX AND YOU’D WALK RIGHT BY HIM.” Below the quote, “ROB REINER” is credited. In the bottom left corner, there is a small red logo with the word “TRUMP” visible.

The image shows a side profile of Donald Trump against a dark background. Below his image is a quote attributed to Maureen Dowd, criticizing a political candidate for emotional behavior and hair care concerns. The initials "NJ" are visible in the top left corner.

The image is a four-panel meme featuring political figures. In the top-left panel, a man is shown with the text "WHAT HAVE I DONE?" below him, while the top-right panel shows another man with "DEAR GOD" beneath. The bottom-left panel repeats "WHAT HAVE I" and the bottom-right panel displays "DONE?" in large letters.

The image features a close-up of Donald Trump giving a thumbs-up gesture. Text at the top reads "DID YOU KNOW?" and at the bottom, "OUT OF 3 TRUMP SUPPORTERS AT LEAST AS STUPID AS THE OTHER," with the latter phrase partially cut off. The background shows a brick wall.

The image is a political meme posted by "Douglas County Republicans," featuring a cutout of Hillary Clinton pointing with the text "YOU GET BACK IN THAT HOUSE! WE'RE TRYING TO CONTROL ANOTHER ELECTION." Below, it lists "THREE BIG REASONS Dems want to extend quarantine," including "Mail in ballots so they can cheat," "Keep Demantia Joe off the campaign trial," and "Crash the economy and blame Trump."

The image features a close-up of Mark Wahlberg with a confused and concerned facial expression. Text overlaid at the top reads “THAT FACE YOU MAKE,” and additional text at the bottom says, “WHEN SOMEONE YOU THOUGHT WAS SMART SAYS TRUMP WILL MAKE A GOOD PRESIDENT.” The phrase “OCCUPY DEMOCRATS” is also visible in small text on the left side.

The image shows a digitally altered photo of Donald Trump wearing a black cowboy hat and holding a large knife, with a fierce expression. White bold text overlays the image, reading: "TELL CLINTON I'M COMING!!" and "AND THE DEPLORABLES ARE COMING WITH ME!" The background is dark and indistinct.

The image shows a smiling woman with short blonde hair, wearing a dark blazer, a pearl necklace, and a ring. She is looking down and to her right with a joyful expression. Text at the top reads "WHERE'S HILLARY?" and at the bottom "COUNTING VOTES!"

The image shows two people, likely medical professionals given their stethoscopes, standing before a whiteboard. One person is pointing at a graph on the board, which has red and blue lines and the handwritten text "15 days to flatten the curve." The image includes a headline that reads, "Experts Call For 15 Days Of Counting To Flatten The Curve Of Votes For Trump," with a website attribution at the bottom.

The image shows Donald Trump speaking at a podium with an American flag in the background. He is raising his right hand, and text overlays read "I PICKED PENCE" and "BECAUSE HIS NAME RHYMES WITH FENCE."

A man in a suit, identified as Joe Biden, is wiping his eyes with his hand, appearing emotional. Behind him, another man in a formal military uniform stands at attention. An American flag is partially visible in the background.

The image shows two men in suits seated in an ornate room, likely an office or formal setting. On the left, a man rests his chin on his hand, looking down. On the right, another man gestures with both hands as if speaking. Text overlaid at the top humorously depicts a dialogue between them.

The image shows two men in suits, one kissing the cheek of the other, with an American flag background. The man being kissed has blonde hair and is wearing a dark suit with a red tie, while the man doing the kissing has white hair and wears a dark suit with a blue tie. The scene is framed with overlaid text that appears to be a humorous dialogue between the two individuals.

The image shows a digitally altered scene where four women are holding and struggling to change a shirtless man's diaper. The man's face is superimposed onto the body of a man in a diaper, and the women appear to be exerting effort. Text at the top and bottom reads, “HURRY WE HAVE TO CHANGE HIS DIAPER” and “HIS DEBATE IS IN FIVE MINUTES!” respectively.

The image features a man in a dark suit and red tie seated at a desk with a blue background containing faint text. He is gesturing with his right hand while looking forward. The image includes text at the top and bottom reading, “First man in history to win an argument with a woman.”

The image shows a man with white hair, wearing a dark suit jacket over a light blue collared shirt, speaking at a podium with a microphone. Large white text overlaid on the image reads, “I DON’T KNOW WHERE I AM.” The background is out of focus, suggesting an outdoor setting.

The image shows a side-profile photograph of Joe Biden in a dark suit, gesturing with his right hand while speaking on a stage with purple lighting. Below the photo is a news headline from Daily Wire News dated October 5, 2020, attributing a quote to Biden about police support and sensitivity training. The photo is credited to Roberto Schmidt/AFP via Getty Images.

The image is a two-panel meme featuring actor Jim Carrey in the top panel with the text "JUST SAY YES TO SOCIALISM" and his name below. The bottom panel shows the character played by Jim Carrey in "Dumb and Dumber," with the text "DUMB AND DUMBER WAS A MOVIE... NOT A LIFESTYLE" overlaid.

The image shows President Joe Biden and Pope Francis standing and conversing in what appears to be a grand, ornate interior, likely a church or cathedral. Biden, dressed in a dark suit, is facing the Pope, who is wearing white papal robes and glasses. Other individuals, including a cardinal in red vestments, are visible in the background.

An elderly woman with white hair is painting a portrait on an easel in what appears to be an art gallery. Several framed paintings hang on the gray walls behind her, and text labels are superimposed on the image: "terrorists" on one painting, "antifa" on the painting she is working on, and "liberals" on her sweater. The gallery has wooden flooring and small white placards next to the artwork.

This is a four-panel meme featuring actress Jenna Coleman and actor Peter Capaldi. In the first panel, Jenna Coleman asks, “Is four years a lot?” The subsequent panels show Peter Capaldi replying, “Depends on the context,” then elaborating, “if Trump wins, no.” and “if Biden wins, yes.”

The image is a three-panel meme featuring former U.S. President Barack Obama in each panel, labeled 2016, 2019, and 2020. Each panel shows him with a similar expression while displaying text that reflects on the state of the economy and political blame. The text in each panel is attributed to Obama and presents a progressively self-contradictory or shifting narrative about economic responsibility.

The image is a meme featuring a photograph of Joe Biden pointing to the side, with two speech bubbles. One bubble reads, “SO WHAT IF DONALD TRUMP CREATED MILLIONS OF NEW JOBS?”, and the other reads, “MY SUPPORTERS DON’T WANT JOBS!” A banner at the bottom promotes “PATRIOTPOST.US.”

A small tan dog wearing a green harness is standing on a paved surface, lifting its leg to defecate directly onto the cover of a TIME magazine featuring Donald Trump. The magazine’s headline reads “Is Trump President?” and text overlays label the scene as “STAGE #1 - ACTION COMPLETE” and “STAGE #2 MAKE IT STICK!”

The image features a close-up of Donald Trump with a pouty, assertive facial expression. Bold, white, capitalized text overlays the image, reading "WHO EVER YOU ARE TALKING ABOUT" at the top and "MUST BE FROM MEXICO" at the bottom. He is wearing a dark suit and white shirt with a light-colored tie.
